title: Make event handlers idempotent
description: Assume at-least-once delivery and design handlers so processing the same event twice produces one effect, using an idempotency key.
body: Use when a function processes events from a queue, stream, or webhook. Do not assume exactly-once delivery, and do not perform a non-idempotent side effect (charge, insert, send) without a dedup guard. Actions: derive a stable idempotency key from the event, record processed keys in a store with a conditional write, and short-circuit duplicates; make downstream writes upserts where possible. Validation signals include a replay test that delivers the same event twice and asserts a single effect. Risk boundary: a double-charge or duplicate side effect from a retry is a correctness and sometimes financial bug; treat missing idempotency on money/state-changing handlers as blocking.
